handbook_series: Book 11 in the series of handbooks

contact.postal_address: University of Cape Town, Private Bag X3, 7701 Rondebosch
contact.deans_office: Chris Hani Building, Level 6, University Avenue North Road, Upper Campus

course_code_system.description: The BSc curriculum is based on a semester system where a semester course equals a half-year of academic study.

F.note: Can ONLY be taken in Semester 1 (Feb-Jun). Must register at the beginning of the year.

S.note: Can ONLY be taken in Semester 2 (Jul-Nov). Cannot be started at the beginning of the year.

H.meaning: Course taught over the whole year, generally with the credit value of a single semester course
H.note: Runs the full year but carries half-credit (same as a semester course). Must register at the beginning of the year. H courses in the EDP may be 'intensive type' — half credit but full contact time over the whole year.

W.meaning: Course taught over the whole year (full credit)
W.note: Runs the full year with full-year credit. Must register at the beginning of the year — CANNOT be joined mid-year.

Z.meaning: Special allocation or any other combination
Z.note: Non-standard scheduling. Check departmental entry for specific timing.

P.note: Intensive course run during the November-December summer term.

L.note: Intensive course run during the June-July winter term.

breakdown.000: Course number — distinguishes from other first-year Chemistry courses

breakdown.002: Course number — distinguishes from other third-year Biology courses

course_code_system.year_level_note: Second-year and third-year courses are usually regarded as 'senior courses' in terms of meeting curriculum requirements for the BSc.

essential_terminology.prerequisite: A course required to be COMPLETED AND PASSED before registering for another course. Where prerequisite courses are failed or not completed, the follow-on course MUST be deregistered.
essential_terminology.corequisite: A course that must be registered for at the SAME TIME as another course.
essential_terminology.dp: Duly Performed certificate - earning the right to sit the final examination by meeting coursework requirements.
essential_terminology.dpr: Duly Performed Refused - student is NOT eligible to sit the examination.
essential_terminology.sub_minimum: A minimum mark that must be achieved in coursework and/or the final examination.
essential_terminology.nqf_credits: National Qualifications Framework credits. A semester course is typically 18-24 credits; a full-year course typically 36-72 credits.
essential_terminology.senior_courses: Second-year and third-year courses (NQF level 6 and 7 respectively).
essential_terminology.ordinary_examination: The scheduled June or November examinations.

progression_status_codes: Academically eligible to continue - may return the next year
Concession (FEC) to continue - may return the next year, but with specific conditions
Concession (FEC) to change field/specialisation/degree within Faculty
Academically not eligible to continue - may NOT return the next year
Status pending: continue if SUPP/DE exams passed

undergraduate_degrees.name: Bachelor of Science
undergraduate_degrees.note: General BSc with major/minor combinations from Science departments
